Add a "1Password access" doc to GitHub repo https://github.com/u2giants/seafile. `gh` is authed as u2giants. Use the Bash tool (Git Bash/POSIX). Work in C:/repos/temp/1p/seafile. The canonical doc body is at C:/repos/temp/1p/CANONICAL-1password.md — READ IT and use it verbatim. NOTE: this is likely a FORK of a large external project (Seafile), so be least-intrusive.

STEPS:
1. Remove C:/repos/temp/1p/seafile if it exists, then `gh repo clone u2giants/seafile C:/repos/temp/1p/seafile -- --depth 1`. If clone fails or repo is empty, STOP and report.
2. Default branch: `gh repo view u2giants/seafile --json defaultBranchRef --jq .defaultBranchRef.name`. Also check `gh repo view u2giants/seafile --json isFork,parent` to confirm fork status.
3. Read root `*.md` and any `docs/` index. Determine:
   a. MAP: is there a documentation map/index our team maintains (a section/file linking the repo's docs)? On a big upstream fork there usually is NOT one of "ours".
   b. PLACEMENT: because this is a fork of a large external project, prefer a STANDALONE `docs/1password.md` (create `docs/` if needed; keep the `# Using 1Password in this repo` H1) plus ONE concise pointer line near the TOP of README.md (e.g. a short blockquote: "> Internal: see [docs/1password.md](docs/1password.md) for 1Password access (MCP server + op CLI)."). Do NOT restructure or rewrite the upstream README.
4. Write the doc and add the single pointer line.
5. If there is genuinely no "our" documentation map, FLAG "NO MAP" in your report (a big upstream fork having no internal map is expected).
6. DELIVERY:
   a. Create branch `docs/1password-access` from the default branch; commit ONLY the doc + the one-line README pointer. Message: `docs: add 1Password MCP + op CLI usage guide` with trailer `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>`. Push; open a PR vs the default branch (`gh pr create`, body ends with `🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)`).
   b. IF push/PR is blocked by a repository ruleset forbidding new branches (rejection mentioning ruleset / "cannot create ref" / GH013 / protected) → FALL BACK: checkout the default branch, commit the same changes there, push the default branch. Report you used a DIRECT COMMIT and why.
   c. If BOTH fail, report the exact error.
7. Modify nothing else. No real secret values.

RETURN terse: repo; fork? (yes/no); placement (path + pointer location); map status ("NO MAP — flagged" if none); delivery (PR URL, or "direct commit to <branch>" + commit URL); any blockers.

1	# Using 1Password in this repo
2	
3	Two ways to pull secrets from 1Password instead of hardcoding them:
4	
5	- **1Password MCP server** — lets AI coding agents (Claude, Codex, etc.) read and manage 1Password items through tools.
6	- **`op` CLI** — lets humans, scripts, and CI read secrets and inject them into environment variables.
7	
8	Both authenticate with a **1Password Service Account token** and use **secret references** of the form `op://<vault>/<item>/<field>`.
9	
10	> **Never commit secrets.** Store them in 1Password and reference them with `op://...`. Keep real `.env` files out of git.
11	
12	## 1. 1Password MCP server (for AI clients)
13	
14	`@u2giants/1password-mcp` is an MCP server that exposes 1Password tools to MCP-compatible clients (Claude Desktop, Claude Code, VS Code, OpenAI Codex, etc.).
15	
16	Add it to your client config (example for Claude Desktop / Claude Code / VS Code):
17	
18	```json
19	{
20	  "mcpServers": {
21	    "1password": {
22	      "command": "npx",
23	      "args": ["-y", "@u2giants/1password-mcp"],
24	      "env": {
25	        "OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN": "<your-service-account-token>"
26	      }
27	    }
28	  }
29	}
30	```
31	
32	OpenAI Codex (TOML), referencing an env var instead of storing the token in config:
33	
34	```toml
35	[mcp_servers."1password"]
36	command = "npx"
37	args = ["-y", "@u2giants/1password-mcp"]
38	env_vars = ["OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN"]
39	```
40	
41	Once connected, the agent has tools including:
42	
43	- `vault_list`, `item_list`, `item_lookup`, `item_get`
44	- `password_read` (read a secret by `op://` reference or vault/item id)
45	- `password_create`, `password_update`, `password_generate`, `password_generate_memorable`
46	- `item_edit`, `item_delete`, `item_archive`, `note_create`
47	
48	Source & full docs: https://github.com/u2giants/1Password-MCP (npm: `@u2giants/1password-mcp`).
49	
50	## 2. `op` CLI (for humans, scripts, and CI)
51	
52	Install the 1Password CLI (`op`): https://developer.1password.com/docs/cli/get-started/
53	
54	Authenticate. For automation, use a Service Account token:
55	
56	```bash
57	export OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN="<your-service-account-token>"   # macOS/Linux
58	```
59	```powershell
60	$env:OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN = "<your-service-account-token>"   # Windows PowerShell
61	```
62	
63	(Interactive desktop users can instead run `op signin`.)
64	
65	Common commands:
66	
67	```bash
68	# Read a single secret to stdout
69	op read "op://<vault>/<item>/<field>"
70	
71	# List vaults / items
72	op vault list
73	op item list --vault "<vault>"
74	op item get "<item>" --vault "<vault>"
75	
76	# Run a command with secrets injected as env vars (values never touch disk)
77	op run -- <your-command>
78	
79	# Fill a template: .env.tpl contains FOO=op://vault/item/field
80	op inject -i .env.tpl -o .env
81	```
82	
83	Use `op://` references in committed templates (e.g. `.env.tpl`) and resolve them at runtime with `op run` / `op inject`, so real secret values never land in git.
84	
85	### Secret reference format
86	
87	```
88	op://<vault>/<item>/<field>
89	op://<vault>/<item>/<section>/<field>
90	```
91

1	# seafile — POP Creations
2	
3	Infrastructure for the Seafile Pro file sync deployment serving POP Creations designers.
4	
5	8 designers in São Paulo access a file library that lives on Synology NAS devices in a NYC office. This repo contains everything to operate both sides of the system.
6	
7	## Structure
8	
9	```
10	seafile-server/               Linode VPS — live at seafile.designflow.app
11	├── seafile-server.yml        Docker Compose: Seafile, MariaDB, Redis
12	├── caddy.yml                 Docker Compose: Caddy reverse proxy + TLS
13	├── nas-settings.yml          Docker Compose: NAS sync settings web panel
14	├── .env.example              Environment variable template (never commit .env)
15	├── nas-settings/             Flask app — seaf-cli control panel + ingest/schedule settings
16	└── custom-templates/         Seahub template overrides (Microsoft login button + NAS Sync sidebar links)
17	
18	synology-seaf-cli/            NYC Synology NAS — running on edgesynology1
19	├── Dockerfile                Wrapper image built on flrnnc/seafile-client
20	├── entrypoint.py             Fixed Seafile daemon entrypoint (replaces upstream default)
21	├── seaf-entrypoint.py        Legacy staging wrapper kept for workflow/test compatibility
22	├── docker-compose.yml        Single NAS seaf-cli container with multi-library mounts
23	├── .env.example              NAS sync credentials template
24	└── README.md                 Synology setup and redeploy instructions
25	
26	windows-workstation/          Windows rendering machine — seaf-cli + PopDAM agent
27	├── docker-compose.yml        seaf-cli containers (sources via CIFS from NAS over LAN)
28	├── setup.ps1                 One-shot installer: PopDAM agent + Docker + seaf-cli
29	└── README.md                 Machine replacement instructions
30	
31	.github/workflows/
32	├── seaf-cli-image.yml        Lint + test + build + push seaf-cli wrapper image to GHCR
33	└── nas-settings-image.yml    Lint + test + build + push nas-settings panel image to GHCR

35	docs/
36	├── architecture.md           System design, containers, data flow, storage
37	├── configuration.md          All env vars, config files, and their meanings
38	├── deployment.md             Start/stop, updates, backup, DNS, NAS image releases
39	└── development.md            Logs, debugging, API usage, user management
40	```

1	# AGENTS.md - POP Creations Seafile Infrastructure
2	
3	## Project summary
4	
5	This repo operates POP Creations' Seafile Pro deployment at `seafile.designflow.app`: NYC Synology NAS design folders are uploaded by `seaf-cli` containers, stored in Linode Object Storage, and accessed by POP Creations staff through Seafile web/desktop clients. The important moving parts are the VPS Docker stack, the NAS sync containers, the `nas-settings` admin panel, Microsoft Entra SSO, and repo-driven CI images; the outcome that matters is reliable, auditable NAS-to-Seafile sync with safe admin controls.
6	
7	## Multi-model AI note
8	
9	There is no universal ignore-file standard across AI coding tools.
10	
11	`.claudeignore` works for Claude Code.
12	
13	When using any other AI tool, paste this file as your first message and follow the instructions in the "What to ignore" section.
14	
15	## Documentation map: what to read for each task
16	
17	Always start with:
18	
19	- `AGENTS.md`
20	
21	Then load additional docs only when relevant:
22	
23	| Task / question | Read these docs | Usually do not need |
24	|---|---|---|
25	| Quick repo orientation | `README.md`, `AGENTS.md` | Deep docs under `docs/` unless task requires them |
26	| Modify app behavior or project-owned code | `AGENTS.md`, relevant folder-level `README.md`, `docs/architecture.md` if system design is affected | `docs/deployment.md` unless deploy behavior changes |
27	| Add or change configuration, env vars, feature flags, secrets, or runtime settings | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, `docs/deployment.md` if prod/runtime env is affected | Unrelated architecture docs |
28	| Change local setup, dev scripts, test/lint/debug workflow, package scripts, or tooling | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/development.md`, relevant package/config files | `docs/deployment.md` unless CI/CD changes |
29	| Change deployment, Docker, CI/CD, hosting, release flow, rollback, or runtime environment | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/deployment.md`, `docs/configuration.md`, relevant workflow/deployment files | Local-only development docs unless needed |
30	| Change database schema, migrations, models, external IDs, or data flow | `AGENTS.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/configuration.md` if env/config is affected, relevant migration/model docs | Deployment docs unless rollout/deploy behavior changes |
31	| Investigate bugs or incidents | `AGENTS.md`, relevant docs based on affected area, `HANDOFF.md` if present, Critical incidents section in `AGENTS.md` | Unrelated folder-level READMEs |
32	| Continue unfinished work | `AGENTS.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, relevant docs named inside `HANDOFF.md` | Docs unrelated to the handoff scope |
33	| Work in a subfolder with its own README | `AGENTS.md`, that folder-level `README.md`, and only broader docs referenced there | Other folder-level READMEs |
34	| Claude Code session | `CLAUDE.md`, then `AGENTS.md` | Other docs unless task requires them |
35	| Documentation-only cleanup | `AGENTS.md`, `README.md`, affected docs under `docs/`, folder-level READMEs only where relevant | Source files except as needed to verify accuracy |
36	
37	`HANDOFF.md` is required reading when it exists. It is temporary continuation context, not canonical architecture.
38	
39	## Repository structure
40	
41	Project-owned code:
42	
43	- `seafile-server/nas-settings/` - Flask admin panel and tests for status, controls, config, libraries, ingest windows, sync schedules, and cached folder-size display.
44	- `synology-seaf-cli/` - Docker wrapper image for `seaf-cli`, including staging, daemon supervision, status reporter, command dispatcher, schedule enforcement, and folder-size cache scanner.
45	- `windows-workstation/` - Alternative seaf-cli host for a Windows machine using CIFS source mounts; not active unless explicitly cut over.
46	- `seafile-server/custom-templates/` - Seahub template overrides for the Microsoft login button and admin-only NAS Sync links.
47	
48	Docs:
49	
50	- `README.md` - quick entry point and orientation.
51	- `AGENTS.md` - canonical AI/developer operating guide and documentation router.
52	- `CLAUDE.md` - Claude Code-specific instructions only.
53	- `docs/architecture.md` - system design, components, data flow, constraints.
54	- `docs/development.md` - local setup, run/test/lint/debug workflow.
55	- `docs/configuration.md` - environment variables, config files, feature flags.
56	- `docs/deployment.md` - deploy/release/environment/rollback workflow.
57	- Folder READMEs: `seafile-server/nas-settings/README.md`, `synology-seaf-cli/README.md`, `seafile-server/custom-templates/README.md`, `windows-workstation/README.md`.
58	- `HANDOFF.md` - temporary continuation doc only when work is unfinished, blocked, or partially deployed.
59	
60	Scripts:
61	
62	- `seafile-server/START_SEAFILE.sh` - VPS pre-flight/start helper.
63	- `seafile-server/CONFIGURE_OAUTH.sh` - old one-time Google OAuth script; already applied historically and now obsolete for Microsoft SSO. Do not run.
64	- `seafile-server/CREATE_NAS_SYNC_ACCOUNT.sh` - one-time machine-account script; do not rerun.
65	- `windows-workstation/setup.ps1` - Windows workstation setup script.
66	
67	Deployment files:
68	
69	- `seafile-server/seafile-server.yml`, `seafile-server/caddy.yml`, `seafile-server/nas-settings.yml`.
70	- `synology-seaf-cli/docker-compose.yml`.
71	- `windows-workstation/docker-compose.yml`.
72	- `.github/workflows/seaf-cli-image.yml`, `.github/workflows/nas-settings-image.yml`.
73	
74	Migrations:
75	
76	- No project-owned database migrations exist. Seafile owns its MariaDB schemas (`ccnet_db`, `seafile_db`, `seahub_db`).
77	
78	Generated / third-party / vendor / framework code:
79	
80	- No vendored source tree is committed. Upstream code lives in images: `seafileltd/seafile-pro-mc:13.0-latest`, `flrnnc/seafile-client:latest`, `mariadb:10.11`, `redis`, and `lucaslorentz/caddy-docker-proxy:2.12-alpine`.
81	
82	Build artifacts:
83	
84	- None should be committed. CI publishes images to GHCR; local Python caches and secret dumps are ignored.
85	
86	## Prime Directive: custom-code boundary
87	
88	Our custom code lives here:
89	
90	- `seafile-server/nas-settings/`
91	- `synology-seaf-cli/`
92	- `windows-workstation/`
93	- `seafile-server/custom-templates/`
94	- `seafile-server/*.yml`
95	- `seafile-server/*.sh`
96	- `docs/`
97	- folder-level `README.md` files
98	- `.github/workflows/`
99	
100	Everything else requires justification before touching. Do not edit Seafile's installed upstream files inside containers as the production change. Seahub UI changes belong in `seafile-server/custom-templates/`; runtime config changes must be documented and backed up.
101	
102	## Core modification inventory
103	
104	| File | Change made | Why it was necessary | Risk during upgrades |

189	## Intentional quirks and non-obvious decisions
190	
191	### Cloudflare proxy disabled
192	
193	Looks like:
194	The public host should be behind Cloudflare orange-cloud proxy.
195	
196	Actually:
197	`seafile.designflow.app` is DNS-only.
198	
199	Why:
200	Seafile desktop sync can use protocols/paths that do not work reliably through Cloudflare's HTTP proxy layer.
201	
202	Do not change because:
203	Proxying the record can break desktop sync clients.
204	
205	### seaf-cli wrapper image
206	
207	Looks like:
208	`ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest` is an unnecessary wrapper around `flrnnc/seafile-client`.
209	
210	Actually:
211	The wrapper adds `tini`, fixed process supervision, a correct healthcheck, stale PID/socket cleanup, status reporting, command dispatch, schedule enforcement, and cached folder-size scanning.
212	
213	Why:
214	The upstream community image has caused silent sync failures and does not provide this deployment's admin bridge.
215	
216	Do not change because:
217	Using the upstream image directly removes production safeguards and the NAS Settings control loop.
218	
219	### NAS `/library/<key>` paths are live bind mounts
220	
221	Looks like:
222	`/library` is a disposable staging volume from the older wrapper design.
223	
224	Actually:
225	The current NAS compose bind-mounts live NAS folders directly under `/library/<key>` in the single `seaf-cli` container. The named `seaf-cli-data` volume is only for seaf-daemon state and caches.
226	
227	Why:
228	The current wrapper supports multi-library sync directly through `SEAF_LIBRARY_<KEY>` variables and Seafile's exact `seafile-ignore.txt` filename.
229	
230	Do not change because:
231	Deleting `/library` paths or treating them as cache can affect live NAS data. To force sync-state rebuilds, operate on `seaf-cli-data` only after an explicit rollback/recovery plan.
232	
233	### Sync schedule has weekday and weekend windows
234	
235	Looks like:
236	The schedule is a single days/start/end block.
237	
238	Actually:
239	The panel writes a schedule with separate `weekdays` and `weekends` windows, a shared timezone, and an outer `enabled` flag. The NAS agent still accepts the older one-window shape for backward compatibility.
240	
241	Why:
242	Business-hour and off-hour sync needs differ between weekdays and weekends, and overnight windows are common.
243	
244	Do not change because:
245	Collapsing the shape back to one window removes the requested granularity and can make existing settings ambiguous.
246	
247	### `nas-settings` cannot push commands to the NAS
248	
249	Looks like:
250	The VPS panel should directly call the NAS.
251	
252	Actually:
253	The NAS containers poll `/api/status` every 30 seconds and receive queued commands in the response.
254	
255	Why:
256	The VPS cannot reliably reach the NAS, and commands need to route by library UUID rather than ephemeral container hostnames.
257	
258	Do not change because:
259	Direct push assumptions break remote operation; hostname-keyed commands can miss the target container.
260	
261	### `/api/settings` is unauthenticated
262	
263	Looks like:
264	The endpoint is missing auth.
265	
266	Actually:
267	It intentionally exposes only non-secret library UUID/settings data so NAS containers can poll without a Seafile admin session.
268	
269	Why:
270	The NAS agent needs ingest/schedule settings before it can report authenticated status; write/admin endpoints remain protected.
271	
272	Do not change because:
273	Adding browser-session auth would break the NAS agent's hourly settings refresh.
274	
275	### NAS deploy uses SSH from the VPS
276	
277	Looks like:
278	GitHub Actions or the NAS MCP should recreate containers after image publish, or the old per-library container names should be managed directly.
279	
280	Actually:
281	CI only publishes images. From the VPS, `ssh edge1` logs in to `edgesynology1` as `ai`; Docker requires `sudo -n /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker`. The live NAS deployment is the single `seaf-cli` container from `synology-seaf-cli/docker-compose.yml`.
282	
283	Why:
284	There is no deploy platform. The `ai` account has a narrow sudoers drop-in for the Synology Docker binary, and old staged files such as `/tmp/seaf-cli-compose.yml` may still describe obsolete per-library containers.
285	
286	Do not change because:
287	Using stale two-service compose files or old container names can recreate the wrong topology. Stage the current compose file and verify `docker compose config --services` shows only `seaf-cli` before running `up`.
288	
289	### Verify the live VPS before deploying
290	
291	What changed:
292	On 2026-06-16, a deployment attempt first landed on the wrong host (`root@hetz`, Tailscale IP `100.66.37.58`) where `/worksp/seafile` was a stale repo clone and no Seafile containers existed.
293	
294	Why:
295	The live public host resolves to `172.233.14.233` and should identify as `seafile-br`; `/opt/seafile` holds the runtime `.env` and base compose files, while `nas-settings.yml` is deployed from `/home/ai/seafile-repo/seafile-server/nas-settings.yml`.
296	
297	Future sessions should:
298	Before running Docker deploy commands, confirm `hostname`, `ls -la /opt/seafile`, and `docker ps` show `seafile`, `seafile-caddy`, and `nas-settings`. If those are absent, stop and find the live VPS instead of adapting commands to the wrong machine.
299	
300	### seaf-cli failed clone tasks self-heal
301	
302	What changed:
303	On 2026-06-11, Character Licensed hit Seafile's "too many files" sync limit and left a failed clone task in `/seafile/seafile-data/clone.db`. Commit `b3436f7` added wrapper cleanup for failed clone tasks before retrying `seaf-cli sync`.
304	
305	Why:
306	After the server-side limit was raised, seaf-daemon still returned "Task is already in progress" for the stale failed clone task.
307	
308	Future sessions should:
309	Do not delete the `seaf-cli-data` volume for this failure mode. Confirm the server has the intended fileserver limits, then let the current image clear only `state=error` clone rows for the affected repo; active fetch/upload tasks are intentionally left alone.
310	
311	### 2026-06-19 Generic Decor false-synchronized state
312	
313	What changed:
314	Generic Decor drift was traced to Synology host inotify exhaustion, not a stale Seafile index. `seaf-daemon` logged `fail to add watch` / `No space left on device` while the host limit was still `fs.inotify.max_user_watches=8192`; the synced trees contain roughly 541k directories, about 82% of them `@eaDir` thumbnail/metadata junk.
315	
316	Why:
317	When directories are not watched, NAS edits there do not fire events, so `seaf-cli status` can report `synchronized` because the client index sees no change. Restarting `seaf-daemon` runs a one-time scan and can temporarily mask the symptom while leaving the inotify limit broken.
318	
319	Future sessions should:
320	Do not treat restart as the repair. Raise the Synology host inotify limits persistently via a DSM boot-up task, then deploy the updated `synology-seaf-cli` image. Seafile ignore files must be named exactly `seafile-ignore.txt`; `.seafile-ignore` is not recognized. Ignore rules are cleanup/hygiene and may not reduce inotify watches.
321	
322	### seaf-cli "bugs" reported against this image are usually base-image bugs, already fixed here
323	
324	What changed:
325	A 2026-06 review reported three seaf-cli container bugs — `healthcheck()` always exits 0 (no return), `seaf-daemon` zombie / `tail -f` parking with no restart and no tini, and an unconditional `chown -R seafile:seafile /seafile /library`. Verified against source: all three live in the upstream base image `flrnnc/seafile-client` (its `/entrypoint.sh` and `/home/seafile/entrypoint.py`), NOT in upstream Seafile's `seaf-cli`/`seaf-daemon`, and NOT in this wrapper.
326	
327	Why:
328	This image's `Dockerfile` overrides `ENTRYPOINT` to `tini -- /home/seafile/entrypoint.py` (our own `synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py`), bypassing the base `/entrypoint.sh` entirely. Our entrypoint already fixes/bypasses all three: `healthcheck()` returns `0/1` and guards a missing rpc socket; `watch()` polls the daemon PID and exits non-zero so Docker restarts it; SIGTERM/SIGINT call `seaf-cli stop`; tini is PID 1; and no `chown -R` runs (no base shell wrapper executes).
329	
330	Future sessions should:
331	Do not re-fix those three — they are not present in the running image. Verify against `synology-seaf-cli/entrypoint.py` + `Dockerfile`, not against the `flrnnc/seafile-client` base. The only genuine wrapper bug from that review was the ignore filename/timing (`.seafile-ignore`, written only on first clone), fixed in 8e0f3c8. The real upstream-Seafile defect is separate and unfixed: the worktree monitor (`wt-monitor-linux.c`) adds inotify watches to ignored directories too, so `seafile-ignore.txt` does not reduce inotify watch count.
332	
333	### seaf-cli deploys ONLY from /volume1/docker/seaf-cli with a .env (2026-06-21 incident)
334	
335	What changed:
336	The seaf-cli stack is canonically deployed at `/volume1/docker/seaf-cli/` on edgesynology1 — `docker-compose.yml` (from `synology-seaf-cli/`) + a `.env` (chmod 600) loaded via `env_file:`. It is in Watchtower's watch list (`synology-monitor` `deploy/synology/docker-compose.agent.yml`).
337	
338	Why:
339	It had drifted to a personal home dir with creds in a non-auto-loaded `seaf-cli.env`. `sudo docker compose up -d` (no `--env-file`) recreated the container with an empty env — `sudo` strips exported shell vars — so it started with no login (`Bad configuration: SEAF_USERNAME required`) and crash-looped. It was also outside Watchtower's scope, so image fixes never auto-deployed.
340	
341	Future sessions should:
342	Never deploy seaf-cli from a home dir or `/tmp`, and never pass creds via shell env + `sudo`. Use the `.env` in the stack dir (`env_file:` fails loud if missing). Keep `seaf-cli` in Watchtower's command list. The repo compose is the source of truth — copy it as-is; do not hand-maintain `-codex`/`-tmp` variants. Full detail in `synology-seaf-cli/README.md` → "2026-06-21 incident".
343	
344	### Seahub template overrides are full-file copies
345	
346	Looks like:
347	Large copied templates should be avoided.
348	
349	Actually:
350	They are the only available hook for login-button branding and custom sidebar links.
351	
352	Why:
353	Seafile does not expose a plugin slot for these UI changes.
354	
355	Do not change because:
356	Editing installed Seahub files would be untracked; removing the overrides removes the only visible panel entry points.
357	
358	### Folder sizes are cached, not calculated live
359	
360	Looks like:
361	The file browser should compute folder sizes when you browse a directory.
362	
363	Actually:
364	The NAS agent computes recursive source-folder sizes in the background and reports a cache to the Libraries page.
365	
366	Why:
367	Live recursive walks over hundreds of thousands of files and multi-terabyte folders would be slow and disruptive.
368	
369	Do not change because:
370	Live folder-size calculation would make browsing expensive and unreliable.
371	
372	### Microsoft SSO uses internal `@auth.local` usernames
373	
374	Looks like:
375	The `@auth.local` accounts are fake users that should be renamed to human emails.
376	
377	Actually:
378	They are Seafile internal usernames for SSO-created users; human identity is in profile/contact email and OAuth bindings.
379	
380	Why:
381	Seafile's OAuth flow creates internal user IDs and maps external identities separately.
382	
383	Do not change because:
384	Renaming internal IDs casually can orphan OAuth bindings, library ownership, or profile rows.

463	## Deployment
464	
465	Real deployment path:
466	
467	- Branch: `main`.
468	- CI workflow `seaf-cli image`: `.github/workflows/seaf-cli-image.yml`; runs pyflakes and `synology-seaf-cli/test_entrypoint.py`, then publishes `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest` and `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:sha-<commit>`.
469	- CI workflow `nas-settings image`: `.github/workflows/nas-settings-image.yml`; runs pyflakes and `seafile-server/nas-settings/test_app.py`, then publishes `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:nas-settings-latest` and `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:nas-settings-sha-<commit>`.
470	- Deploy trigger: manual pull/recreate on the target host after CI succeeds. There is no Coolify, deploy webhook, or app/project ID.
471	- VPS stack: Docker Compose from `/opt/seafile` plus repo files in `/home/ai/seafile-repo/seafile-server/`.
472	- `nas-settings` deploy: from `/opt/seafile`, run compose with `--env-file /opt/seafile/.env -f /home/ai/seafile-repo/seafile-server/nas-settings.yml`; do not build on the VPS.
473	- NAS deploy: SSH from the VPS with `ssh edge1`, stage the current `synology-seaf-cli/docker-compose.yml` as `/tmp/seaf-cli-compose-codex.yml` or another verified current path, rebuild `/tmp/.env` from the running `seaf-cli` container if needed, pull `ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest`, then run Docker Compose with `sudo -n /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker`.
474	- Rollback: pin the affected service image to an older immutable `sha-<commit>` or `nas-settings-sha-<commit>` tag and recreate.
475	- Runtime environment variable values live in `/opt/seafile/.env` on the VPS and `/tmp/.env` on the NAS. `/tmp` is wiped on NAS reboot; running containers survive via Docker restart policy, but recreates may need restaging.
476	- SSH is allowed for manual operator work on VPS/NAS. SSH is not a CI deployment path; GitHub Actions must not SSH to production or run Docker there.
477	
478	See `docs/deployment.md` for command details.
479	
480	## Critical incidents
481	
482	### 2026-06-05 NAS seaf-cli containers found removed
483	
484	What happened:
485	The NAS sync containers were absent from `docker ps -a`, so sync had silently stopped.
486	
487	Impact:
488	NAS-to-Seafile uploads were not running. No data loss was documented; Seafile libraries, S3 data, and Docker volumes remained.
489	
490	Root cause:
491	Unknown. Docker event history did not retain the removal event. Possible manual removal, Synology Container Manager behavior, or NAS maintenance.
492	
493	Recovery:
494	Containers were recreated from `synology-seaf-cli/docker-compose.yml`.
495	
496	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
497	Verify live container state before documenting sync status; do not trust stale status claims in docs.
498	
499	### 2026-06-07 SSO admin binding pointed at a duplicate account
500	
501	What happened:
502	Microsoft SSO for `albert@popcre.com` landed on a non-admin duplicate account instead of the real admin.
503	
504	Impact:
505	The NAS Settings panel and admin-only sidebar link appeared missing because the session was non-admin.
506	
507	Root cause:
508	OAuth binding/account churn in `seahub_db.social_auth_usersocialauth`.
509	
510	Recovery:
511	The binding was repointed to the admin account and duplicate active users were removed. Current active users are the SSO admin and `nas-sync` machine account.
512	
513	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
514	If an SSO duplicate appears, fix the binding and account ownership rather than promoting the duplicate. Verify `EmailUser`, `profile_profile`, and `social_auth_usersocialauth` together.
515	
516	### 2026-06-07 nas-settings redirected admins because internal URL used Seahub `:8000`
517	
518	What happened:
519	Admins visiting `/nas-settings/` were redirected away because the panel's admin-session check could not reach Seahub at `http://seafile:8000`.
520	
521	Impact:
522	The panel was unusable even for admins.
523	
524	Root cause:
525	Seahub gunicorn binds localhost inside the Seafile container; other containers must reach it through nginx on port 80.
526	
527	Recovery:
528	`SEAFILE_INTERNAL_URL` was changed to `http://seafile`.
529	
530	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
531	Use the Seafile container's nginx port 80 for internal panel admin checks, not Seahub's localhost-only port 8000.
532	
533	### 2026-06-08/09 Microsoft OAuth and account cleanup
534	
535	What happened:
536	The generic/no-prompt SSO behavior and later duplicate SSO account state required live Seahub config and database cleanup.
537	
538	Impact:
539	Incorrect SSO behavior risked insecure login flow or admin landing on the wrong account.
540	
541	Root cause:
542	Stale/misconfigured SSO settings and duplicate OAuth bindings/accounts.
543	
544	Recovery:
545	Configured Microsoft Entra OAuth, preserved secrets out of output, transferred NAS library ownership to the SSO admin, kept `nas-sync` as the machine account, removed obsolete active users, and made the two NAS libraries internally visible read-only.
546	
547	Rule added to prevent recurrence:
548	Back up DB/config before destructive account or ownership changes; keep secrets out of output; document account identities by internal username plus contact email.
549
